reviews.review_msg: Even though his lecture seems dry (only from slides and textbook isn't really needed), he willing to help any student and give clear explanation. He also encouraged student to never give up. Definitely will take his class again.

He is super nice. Boring lecture but fair marker. 2 quizzes, both are open book, 1 long term paper (10pages).  Read the textbook and try to relate some concepts to the quizzes and the term paper, you can get a B.

Unorganized, not specific. He gives a double-spaced one page paper assignment every class. It was super easy. I feel like we focused more on biology than we did actual anatomy, and much of the knowledge I attained in my anatomy classes was useless in this class. Good class, but I wish there was more organization.

I am a science student & took it in 2013 as an elective. Lectures are long and get boring most of the time. He dwelled more in East Asia since that's his focus and told a lot of personal stories. 2 tests - 2 or 3 out of 10 ques. - both open book. One long 10pg assignment in the end. Gotta read textbook though although his slides are good too.
Very nice man fairly easy and very knowledgeable.  That said his lectures are so boring I would never ever take another one of his classes.

bit of a boring lecturer, 3 hr classes are hard not to fall asleep in. Very nice prof, has a clear high interest in the material. both textbooks used in the course are his own, not too necessary until papers start as he covers the material well enough in lecture. Tests are open book and fair exams, have loads of time for paper, fair marking.

His textbook is a shotgun blast of world history with no attempt to bring focus on the subject itself, and he never deviates from it. No direction or criteria for tests or papers, which means expectations are low but  frustrating to work with. He doesn't seem to know how to direct learning at all. Good researcher, horrible teacher.

Although 3 hours of listening to his lectures could be boring, but he often presents very fascinating facts about everything he lectures. He is very clear and does not expect much. For both midterms, they were open book, as long as you have read the textbooks and have related the midterms to the main concepts, you are guarantee a good mark!
He is a good prof, he know so much!!! I like his lectures, he does not want much from you - just come to class and understand the main idea of his lecture.

The most knowledgeable person in everything related to Asia, Oceania and the Americas. His hearing is poor, so two-way interaction is problematic. And lecturing is the only way he can communicate. But his narration presents a coherent and fascinating view of the world. He's also been to half the world - from Papua to Russian Far East to Peru.

Incredibly dull. Drones on an on  about Southeast Asia, which is his specialty. Reading is a lot at first, although it gets less. He pretty much follows word for word the reading, which he also wrote. Many found him tacky in terms of style (kaki pants, Hawaiian shirts, etc) and humour. Exams are open-book. Take if you like transnationalism - a lot.

A lot of reading, interesting method of teaching as he just stands there and lectures. However, by standing there and talking he makes you focus more. His testing is open-book, 3 essay questions in 2 hours for two midterms and a 10 page essay. Only take this class if it is required or you are really interested in it.
His interest in the course material seemed very low and the depth to the course was limited. The course has lots of potential but he kept it superficial. He lectures through the tutorials.

Haven't been able to stay awake for one class yet. A lot of the stuff he talks about seems random and pointless. He lied about our map quiz, saying we had to prepare to know 50 locations.. and ended up giving us over 120 !!! He seems like a nice guy and really knows his stuff but I don't think any of us got what we expected out of this course.

Michael is a very nice guy, although he is boring. The two exams are pure memorization. Its not that hard as people make it seem. The paper and presentation are easy and make up for it. Interesting for those who are into SE Asia. I recommend it. People who gave him bad ratings probaly did bad in the course. If you study his material you'll do fine.
very knowledgable and passionate about subject. would be better with more class participation. lots of memorization. not for those uninterested in se asia.

this is the worse class i have taken at SFU. shouldnt be under sa. first quiz is pure memorization of a map of SEA. dont really know what he's talking about in class because theres no text to read before classes until half way though the course. if you miss a lectue you're screwed for the quiz. handouts make no sense unless you go to class.

He is really into SE Asia.  I learned a lot from his class and the cooking presentation was a lot of fun, it was a different experience from other courses.  He's a really nice person and is willing to help you out.  Just be patient with him because he does tend to be boring, but the second half of the semester gets better.

He is boring indeed, but I spent 3 months in SE Asia for Anthropology Field school where we all got to know him as a person, and someone who truly LOVES anthropology. Turned out to be a cool leader. Gotta love the belt always a little too high on the torso!

He is a great prof...can be kinda boring. His tests are hard thought....all memorization...It's crazy. He is really passionate about his work, which is why  you come away learning something new. Overall great prof take him of you have a good memory.

Despite his being an anthropology teacher I found him to be very old-school anth, meaning he is euro-centric (****/****) and makes comments such as "they are wild savages". NOT a good example of an anthropologist.
Tests require you to memorize an abundant of information. Tests are 20% - for the amount of studying required is simply ridiculous.

Do NOT take this course. If he didn't give marks based on attendance, I would never go to this class. Assigned textbook was written by him and is almost completely unrelated to test material. Doesn't tell you what he's looking for in papers/presentations.

HE IS FREAKN BORING!!! His lectures makes no sense and he seems to like useless facts that are not related to the course.  His exams are so freakn hard and impossible to pass but it's a good thing he marks on a bell curve.  Nice guy with no personality.

This class is one of the most boring and useless classes I have ever taken. It is a COMPLETE waste of time, and he is one of the worst lecturers ever. I dont understand why we need to know such useless info. Everyone sleeps and dozes off in this lectures.

Do NOT take this class. Tests are brutal, you just memorize facts and places which are impossible. I didn't learn anything. He tells you what to study for on the test only 2 days in advance. Never responded to my e-mail. He's nice but shouldn't be a prof
Unfair marking scheme but very nice prof.  He helped me all the time and was very approachable.He loves what he does---thats for sure!

Would not recommend this class to anyone.  Marking scheme is very very unfair.  The tests are based on complete memeorization of facts that you do not even understand.  Too many small details and way to much memorization.  The only fun aspect-presentati

Think Mr. Rogers except unhelpful and more dull than usual. The whole class is based on your ability to memorize tedious lists of location names, but you never seem to learn anything about them. This class is a total waste of time and mental effort.
Reaally boring prof but nice guy and super easy marker. Material was strange and mostly facts to memorize but still one of my favorite classes. Good easy credit class.
No Comments

Extremely distant individual who lectures, but does not provide room for discussion or feedback. I spent three months with him in the Southeast Asia field school and only learned that he is as boring overseas as he is on campus.
Worst professor I've had at SFU... lectures  make no sense and have no point, assignments a waste of time
approachable, likeable prof, encouraging more class input would help Dr. Howard.
way too much emphasis on memorization, lectures not helpful at all
totally useless, high school type information and assignments
Very very boring & uninteresting approach. An easy mark if you can stay awake.
